assets.name: lecture_04_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: This frame is the clearest and most balanced of the set. It preserves the main three-step board logic at once: the stationarity equation from the Lagrange-multiplier extremization, the intermediate logarithmic form, and the boxed final result for the canonical distribution. The writing is sharp, the result is finished, and the lecturer's body obscures less of the key equations than in the neighboring candidates.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: Looking at the equation just above z equals equation

assets.name: lecture_04_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: This frame gives the clearest view of the right-hand blackboard where the key thermodynamic identities are written in a clean vertical sequence. The lecturer is far enough left that none of the three main equations is blocked, and the board still retains a little of the preceding boxed entropy formula above, which helps situate the derivation without sacrificing legibility.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: The derivative of S with respect to energy.

assets.name: lecture_04_figure_04.png
assets.rationale: This frame is the strongest documentary choice because the key equations are finished and no longer blocked by the lecturer's pointing hand. It keeps the right-hand result for the energy clearly visible, preserves the lower logarithmic form used to obtain it, and still shows the left-hand integral-approximation dependence on \beta. The neighboring pointing frames obscure the crucial term on the top line, while the last frame moves on to new writing.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: So I'm going to use the integral approximation to say that the energy
